title: Accessing large registers results in excessive compile-time burden (read_verilog)
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Simplified example:

```bash
cat > /tmp/large_register.v <<EOF
module large_register(input  	   clk,
		      input 	   valid,
		      input [19:0] addr,
		      input 	   wr_bit
		      );
   reg [1<<20:0] large_register;
   always @(posedge clk) begin
      large_register[addr] <= (valid ? wr_bit : large_register[addr]);
   end
endmodule
EOF

# Now running yosys on this will eat a lot of CPU and memory
yosys -QTp "read_verilog /tmp/large_register.v"
```
Using version at head: 36df37a734c3dd315f00edbe19b364b6f9a1a132

## Expected behavior

Even though the register in question is somewhat large, the expectation is be that the code would be processed with reasonable use of CPU and memory.

## Actual behavior

Yosys uses 10G+ memory and uses 100% CPU for > 30min (interrupted after that) to just `read_verilog` this code.

# Now running yosys on this will eat a lot of CPU and memory
